title: armeria scabra subsp. labradorica
intro: Armeria scabra subsp. labradorica, also known as the Labrador or Bear's-foot, is a flowering perennial plant native to the rocky and sandy areas of North America. The plant is used medicinally for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as colds, fever, and infections. The health benefits of Armeria scabra subsp. labradorica include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or infused oil, which is often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health and well-being.
